compositional_truth_check.details: P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visually identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only surviving anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.p1_yellow_pot: Systemic Loss: Soil surface is bare; no visible foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage present.
inventory_reconciliation.p3_black_pot: Present: Pothos leaf and rabbit anchor intact.
inventory_reconciliation.p4_shared_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage.

plant_audit.p1: Empty soil, no signs of life.
plant_audit.p2_p4: Soil contains eggshell fragments; no plant matter.
plant_audit.p3: Single Pothos leaf remains; shows signs of chlorosis/thinning.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, potentially over-saturated. No fungal blooms, but significant debris (eggshells).
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of an electronic component (IC chip/header) introduced as a foreign object.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The Pothos leaf in P3 has maintained a consistent, albeit fragile, posture. The soil in P1/P2/P4 has remained static with no new growth. The introduction of the electronic component at T-2 remains the primary environmental change.

visual_health_inference.p3_pothos: Stressed. The leaf is pale, indicating potential light deficiency or root-zone issues.
visual_health_inference.overall_biome: Critical. The lack of active growth across 75% of the registry suggests a failure in the micro-biome or improper substrate conditions.

anomalies: Introduction of a multi-pin electronic header/component on the desk surface.
Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/top-dressing.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of stagnation. The Pothos (P3) is the sole occupant showing biological activity, though it appears weak. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are effectively barren, suggesting either total plant loss or dormancy that has persisted throughout the observation window. The presence of non-botanical debris (eggshells and electronic components) suggests an experimental or non-standard maintenance approach.
